description: Add Three-Tier Evidence Labeling System explanation to the top of the three core methodology files, and apply Tier labels plus all 14 specific claim corrections across the entire knowledge base.
steps: Step 1 - Add the Three-Tier Evidence Labeling System explanation block near the top of core/methodology/songwriting.md (after the 'Overview' section, before Phase 1). The block should define: [Tier 1: Scholarship-backed] = Documented academic framework with primary sources; [Tier 2: Professional heuristic] = Widely-used craft guidance supported by professional practice; [Tier 3: Community/platform heuristic] = Empirical observation from practitioner testing, unverified.

Step 2 - Add the same Three-Tier Evidence Labeling System explanation block near the top of core/methodology/critique.md (after 'Overview: The Multi-Layer Critique Model' section, before 'Prerequisites').

Step 3 - Add the same Three-Tier Evidence Labeling System explanation block near the top of core/methodology/suno-optimization.md (after 'Overview' section, before 'The Complete 13-Step Optimization Procedure'). Add a note: 'Most Suno-specific claims in this document are Tier 3 unless otherwise noted.'

Step 4 - ATTRIBUTION FIX #1: Fix the emotional cue hierarchy attribution. The hierarchy (Mode > Tempo > Register > Dynamics > Articulation > Timbre) must be attributed to Eerola, Friberg, and Bresin (2013), Frontiers in Psychology 4, Article 487 -- NOT 'Juslin' or 'Juslin & Gabrielsson'. Fix in ALL locations: core/methodology/critique.md (line ~309 '| 2 | Key | Juslin (cue hierarchy)', line ~459 'Key is the #1 emotional cue (Juslin)', line ~481 'Patrik Juslin | BRECVEMA emotional mechanisms + cue hierarchy'), core/methodology/suno-optimization.md (section 'Key Optimization Principles' item 2, and 'Theoretical Foundation' table 'Patrik Juslin' row), core/methodology/songwriting.md (line ~528 'Patrik Juslin' in Theoretical Foundation), references/SUNO_STYLE_GENRE_REFERENCE.md (section '2.1 The Emotional Cue Hierarchy (Juslin, Gabrielsson, Frontiers in Psychology)' -- rename to 'Eerola, Friberg & Bresin, 2013', and source table entries for 'Patrik Juslin (2013)' and 'Juslin & Gabrielsson'), references/CRITIQUE_REFERENCE.md (line ~486 'Juslin (cue hierarchy)', ~536 'Key is King -- Mode is the #1 emotional cue (Juslin)', ~556 'Juslin & Gabrielsson (emotional cue research) | Emotional cue hierarchy'), .kiro/steering/suno-formatting.md, .kiro/sops/02-critiquing-a-song.md, .kiro/powers/songwriting/steering/craft.md, README.md. NOTE: Juslin is STILL credited for BRECVEMA (8 emotional mechanisms) -- that remains correct. Only the cue HIERARCHY attribution changes. Separate the two: 'Juslin = BRECVEMA mechanisms' stays, 'cue hierarchy = Eerola, Friberg & Bresin (2013)' replaces.

Step 5 - ATTRIBUTION FIX #2: Change Fabbri's rule labels to his exact terminology everywhere they are listed out. Current: 'Formal, Semiotic, Behavioral, Social/Ideological, Juridical/Commercial'. Correct: 'formal and technical rules, semiotic rules, behaviour rules, social and ideological rules, economical and juridical rules'. Fix in: core/methodology/songwriting.md (line ~84), core/methodology/critique.md (line ~175), references/CRITIQUE_REFERENCE.md (line ~127, ~554), references/SUNO_STYLE_GENRE_REFERENCE.md (section 1.2 table and sources table ~527). NOTE: Brief inline references like 'Fabbri's 5 rules?' in workflow tables can stay as-is -- only the places where the 5 rules are NAMED/LISTED need correction.

Step 6 - ATTRIBUTION FIX #3: Fix Lacasse's proxemics. Add a note that 'dissolved' is OUR adaptation, not Lacasse's original term. The framework is composite: Hall (proxemics theory) + Lacasse (vocal staging semiotics) + Moore (recorded song hermeneutics). Fix in: core/methodology/critique.md (the proxemic distances list under A2, around line ~244 -- add note after 'Dissolved' entry), references/CRITIQUE_REFERENCE.md (the proxemic distances table under A2, around line ~199 -- add note after 'Dissolved' row), core/methodology/character-voice.md (line ~273 mention of dissolved). Also update the Theoretical Foundation tables in critique.md and CRITIQUE_REFERENCE.md for the Lacasse entry to note composite framework.

Step 7 - ATTRIBUTION FIX #4: Fix Moore's 4 layers caveat. Add his own caveat: 'not prescriptive rules, but generalized descriptions' and 'not all will be present in every example'. Change 'Every song has explicit beat...' (in .kiro/powers/songwriting/steering/craft.md) to 'A wide range of popular recordings can be analyzed through...'. Fix in: core/methodology/critique.md (line ~225 'Based on Moore's 4 functional texture layers:'), references/CRITIQUE_REFERENCE.md (line ~169 'Based on Allan F. Moore's 4 functional texture layers in recorded popular song:'), .kiro/powers/songwriting/steering/craft.md (line ~31 'Every song has explicit beat...').

Step 8 - ATTRIBUTION FIX #5: Fix Tagg's musemes definition. Change 'carrying cultural connotation independently of context' or 'carries cultural connotations independent of lyrics' to 'inferred through culturally-informed interobjective comparison'. Fix in: core/methodology/critique.md (line ~256), references/CRITIQUE_REFERENCE.md (line ~213).

Step 9 - CLAIM SOFTENING #6: 'No line over 12 syllables' -> 'Monitor stress density and singability relative to tempo. Lines that feel crowded should be checked for stress alignment, breath points, and performability.' Mark as [Tier 2]. Fix in: core/methodology/songwriting.md (Quality Gates table line ~216, also line ~418 'Lines over 12 syllables | Split or trim -- no exceptions' in Common Mistakes, also lines ~142, ~157, ~197 where '<=12 syllables' appears as checks), .kiro/steering/songwriting.md (line ~60 and ~47), .kiro/sops/01-writing-a-song.md (lines ~106, ~121, ~156, ~284). IMPORTANT: Do NOT remove the check. Soften from absolute rule ('no exceptions') to heuristic check. Keep WARN thresholds in validate-song.py unchanged. The guideline becomes: 'target 7-12 syllables; lines exceeding ~12-15 should be checked for stress density and singability at tempo' rather than hard prohibition.

Step 10 - CLAIM SOFTENING #7: 'Hook within 15 seconds' -> 'Provide a compelling hook signal within the first 5-30 seconds. Full chorus timing is genre-dependent (HSD data: pop avg 39s, hip-hop 30s, #1 hits avg 45s in 2024).' Mark as [Tier 2]. Fix in: core/methodology/songwriting.md (Quality Gates table, Step 8, Step 18), .kiro/steering/songwriting.md (quality gates section). IMPORTANT: Keep the concept of early hook engagement -- soften from absolute 15s to 5-30s range with genre context.

Step 11 - CLAIM SOFTENING #8: 'V2 should never restate V1' -> 'Verse 2 should usually add motion unless reiteration is part of the form's design (blues, hymnal, chant).' Mark as [Tier 2]. Fix in: core/methodology/songwriting.md (Quality Gates table, Common Mistakes table), .kiro/steering/songwriting.md.

Step 12 - CLAIM SOFTENING #9: 'Bridge must shift meaning' -> 'A bridge often works best when it creates contrast or a turn. In some songs that turn is semantic; in others it is musical, textural, or energetic.' Mark as [Tier 2]. Fix in: core/methodology/songwriting.md (Quality Gates, Core Principles #6, Step 14 heading area), .kiro/steering/songwriting.md.

Step 13 - CLAIM SOFTENING #10: 'ABCB is the default' -> 'ABCB is often useful for a conversational feel. One common option, not a universal default.' Mark as [Tier 2]. Fix in: core/methodology/songwriting.md (Quality Gates table, Step 4), .kiro/steering/songwriting.md (quality gates), .kiro/powers/songwriting/steering/craft.md.

Step 14 - SUNO TIER 3 LABELS #11-13: Add Tier 3 notes to Suno claims. (a) '5-8 tags optimal' -> Add: '[Tier 3: Suno v4.5+ docs encourage more descriptive prompting. Test by version.]' in core/methodology/suno-optimization.md Genre Combination Rules section and Key Optimization Principles #7. (b) 'Genre must be first' -> Add: '[Tier 3: community practice, not official. v4.5+ allows conversational prompts.]' in core/methodology/suno-optimization.md Genre Combination Rules section and 7-Dimension table. (c) 'Era > genre labels' -> Add: '[Tier 3: promising but not A/B tested.]' in core/methodology/suno-optimization.md Genre Combination Rules and Key Optimization Principles #5.

Step 15 - KEY-EMOTION TIER SPLIT #14: In core/methodology/suno-optimization.md Key Optimization Principles section item 2 ('Key is King'), and in references/SUNO_STYLE_GENRE_REFERENCE.md section 2.1, add a note splitting: 'Mode effects on perceived valence = [Tier 1: confirmed by Eerola, Friberg & Bresin 2013 and multiple replications]. Specific key character associations (e.g., D minor = devotional, F# minor = mysterious) = [Tier 2: historical tradition (Schubart 1806, Mattheson), not cognitive science. Useful as creative prompts, not empirical claims.]' Also add this split note in references/CRITIQUE_REFERENCE.md where the key-emotion framework is discussed.
acceptance_criteria: Three-tier system explanation appears near the top of songwriting.md, critique.md, and suno-optimization.md
All cue hierarchy references now cite Eerola, Friberg & Bresin (2013) instead of Juslin/Gabrielsson for the hierarchy specifically
Fabbri's rule labels use exact terminology where listed in full
Lacasse proxemics notes 'dissolved' as our adaptation, credits composite framework
Moore's 4 layers includes caveat about generalized descriptions not prescriptive rules
Tagg's musemes uses interobjective comparison language
Syllable, hook timing, V2, bridge, and ABCB claims are softened with Tier 2 labels
Suno claims (5-8 tags, genre first, era anchoring) have Tier 3 labels
Key-emotion table has split tier notation (mode=Tier 1, specific key character=Tier 2)
No quality gates removed -- language softened from rule to check
Validator tool validate-song.py WARN thresholds unchanged
verification: grep -r 'Eerola' /projects/sandbox/songwriting-kb/core/methodology/ to confirm new attribution appears
grep -r 'Tier 1' /projects/sandbox/songwriting-kb/core/methodology/ to confirm tier labels present
grep -r 'Tier 2' /projects/sandbox/songwriting-kb/core/methodology/ to confirm tier labels present
grep -r 'Tier 3' /projects/sandbox/songwriting-kb/core/methodology/suno-optimization.md to confirm Suno tier labels
grep -rn 'dissolved' /projects/sandbox/songwriting-kb/core/methodology/critique.md to confirm note about adaptation
